_meta.name: okta-user-profile-actions
_meta.description: Navigate to a user's profile page and execute lifecycle actions: perceive state, then optionally suspend, deactivate, or reset authenticators. Parameterized by userId.
_meta.verificationNotes: Full end-to-end verified on test user Yeshie Test (00u11x5jinoSKzBC5698). Add-user VERIFIED. Deactivate flow VERIFIED: More Actions -> Deactivate -> OK. NOTE: confirmation button is "OK" not "Deactivate".
anomalies: Admin's own profile shows NO action buttons (Okta self-deactivation protection) — 'More Actions' click_text will fail for the authenticated admin
STAGED users show 'Set Password & Activate' and 'Resend Activation Email' instead of 'Reset Password'
STAGED users also have: Reset Authenticators, Suspend, Deactivate in More Actions dropdown
Unlocked/active users may show 'Reset Password' as a top-level button (unverified)
Profile H1 contains name + email concatenated with newline: 'First Last\n            email@domain.com'
Tabs visible: Applications, Groups, Profile, Devices, Pre-enrolled authenticators (Admin users also get 'Admin roles' tab)
Deactivate confirmation modal uses "OK" button — NOT "Deactivate". click_text("Deactivate") on the modal will fail (text not found).
Old selector [data-se='deactivate-user-btn'] does NOT exist in Okta OIE. Classic UI only.

params.userId: REQUIRED — Okta user ID, format 00u{alphanumeric}698. Get from /admin/users perceive mainActions.

chain.note: Read initial state. Check headings[0].text for name+email. Check mainActions for available actions.
ENABLE to start deactivation. Opens dropdown with: Reset Authenticators, Suspend, Deactivate.
Opens confirmation modal. H2 will be 'Deactivate Person'.
VERIFIED: confirm button is 'OK', NOT 'Deactivate'. click_text('Deactivate') fails here.
Verify deactivation. User should now show DEACTIVATED status.

user-logs-link.description: Link to user's events in system log
